title: trisetum pensylvanicum
intro: Trisetum pensylvanicum, also known as Pennsylvania sedge, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. This plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Trisetum pensylvanicum include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and soothe digestive issues. Trisetum pensylvanicum can be prepared as a tea or infusion, and its dried leaves can also be used in tinctures and salves to harness its medicinal properties.
